prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Oh, sweetheart, well, let me try my best here. From what I understand, the CPU is sort of like the... the brain of the computer, I think that's what your uncle told me once. It does all the thinking and figuring, you know, when you click on something or play one of your games, it's the little part inside that works it all out. I believe CPU stands for something like "Central Processing Unit" — my goodness, what a mouthful! I'm told it's just a small chip, smaller than a cracker, if you can believe that. But listen, dear, if you really want the nitty-gritty details, you might ask your father — he's much better with these modern contraptions than your old grandma is!
